# agentops_review_workflow (Resource)

A code-review workflow that runs reviewer agents against pull requests in one or more GitHub repositories.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Reviewer agents (including built-ins) available to the account.
data "agentops_reviewers" "all" {}

resource "agentops_review_workflow" "backend" {
  name               = "backend-pr-reviews"
  status             = "active"
  base_branch_filter = "main"

  reviewer_agent_ids = [for r in data.agentops_reviewers.all.reviewers : r.agent_id if r.is_builtin]

  repos = [
    {
      repo_owner = "komodorio"
      repo_name  = "mono"
    },
    {
      repo_owner = "komodorio"
      repo_name  = "infra"
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `reviewer_agent_ids` (List of String) IDs of the reviewer agents that run against matching pull requests.

### Optional

- `base_branch_filter` (String) Only review pull requests targeting this base branch. Reviews all branches when omitted.
- `name` (String) Human-readable workflow name. Server-assigned when omitted.
- `repos` (Attributes List) GitHub repositories this workflow reviews. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--repos))
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status. Newly created workflows start as `draft`; set to `active` or `paused` to publish or suspend the workflow. One of `draft`, `active`, `paused`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) Workflow identifier.
- `manual_setup` (Attributes List) Manual webhook setup instructions for repositories the server could not configure automatically. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--manual_setup))
- `repo_count` (Number) Number of repositories configured on the workflow.
- `repo_status` (Attributes List) Observed webhook status for each configured repository. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--repo_status))
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp.
- `webhook_url` (String) Webhook URL GitHub posts pull-request events to.

<a id="nestedatt--repos"></a>
### Nested Schema for `repos`

Required:

- `repo_name` (String) GitHub repository name.
- `repo_owner` (String) GitHub repository owner (user or organization).


<a id="nestedatt--manual_setup"></a>
### Nested Schema for `manual_setup`

Read-Only:

- `content_type` (String) Webhook content type to configure.
- `events` (List of String) GitHub events the webhook should send.
- `repo_name` (String) GitHub repository name.
- `repo_owner` (String) GitHub repository owner.
- `secret` (String, Sensitive) Webhook signing secret. Sensitive.
- `webhook_url` (String) Webhook URL to register in GitHub.


<a id="nestedatt--repo_status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `repo_status`

Read-Only:

- `github_hook_id` (Number) GitHub webhook ID, when provisioned.
- `repo_name` (String) GitHub repository name.
- `repo_owner` (String) GitHub repository owner.
- `webhook_error` (String) Webhook provisioning error, when failed.
- `webhook_status` (String) Webhook provisioning status.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Review workflows are imported by their id.
terraform import agentops_review_workflow.backend rvw_01hxyz
```
